finding_results.notes: Replaced abstract archive taxonomy with a concrete Mercury Night harm record (bodily injury, death time, confidentiality payout coercion), showed Ina's explicit redact/publish boundary choices on the same file, and paired release momentum with immediate embodied response.

Added an external threat cue (hallway voice plus deadbolt handle test) and a defensive action with immediate cost (switch to hotspot, upload slowdown, mirror timeout) before returning to broader replication status.

Removed literal Option A/Option B framing and staged commitment through competing in-scene pressures: legal survivability versus relay continuity and immediate discoverability, then committed through on-camera action.

Rewrote both flagged template constructions without sat/landed-like syntax, using distinct imagery domains: physiological/internal at chapter open and procedural/spatial in takedown-response sequence.

summary: All four pass-scoped structural/craft findings were addressed in chapter 16 with concrete consequence rendering, pressure-driven decision architecture, escalated in-scene threat causality, and de-ticked sentence imagery while preserving continuity constraints and character register.
